linkedin.content:
🚀 We just launched GrantPilot — an AI grant ops marketplace for nonprofits and municipal teams.

After interviewing 47 grant managers, we found the biggest time sink isn't writing — it's knowing what to write. Staff spend 20-40 hours per application just parsing RFPs and tracking requirements before a single word of narrative begins.

Here's what we built:

🔍 RFP Parser — Upload any federal/state/foundation RFP. Get structured requirements, deadlines, scoring rubric, eligibility checklist, and a Win Score in 90 seconds.

🏆 Win Score — 6-factor predictive score (eligibility match, narrative completeness, budget quality, compliance, org profile, QA review) before you commit 40 hours.

✍️ AI Narrative Builder — 58 funder-specific templates + AI generation with your org's voice. Covers CDBG, SAMHSA, NEH, RWJF, MacArthur, DOJ, EPA EJ, and more.

💰 Budget Builder — OMB 2 CFR 200-compliant with personnel, fringe, indirect cost calculations and narrative justifications.

👩‍💼 Human QA Gate — Every submission gets 48-hour specialist review. Insurance-backed SLA.

Small nonprofits spend $3,000–$8,000 per application. We're cutting that by 60%.

Try it free: https://app-limalabs.vercel.app

#nonprofit #grantwriting #AI #socialimpact #GovTech #nonprofittech

We interviewed 47 nonprofit grant managers before writing a line of code. Here's what we learned.

The #1 pain point isn't writing. It's knowing what to write.

Specifically:
• 73% said they waste 20+ hours per application on RFP intake
• 61% said they've started applications only to discover eligibility issues mid-draft
• 84% said they lack confidence in their budget justification narratives
• 67% said they'd pay for expert QA review if it had a fixed price and SLA

The second finding surprised us: the biggest differentiation request wasn't better AI. It was a human expert on the other end.

Grant managers trust AI for drafting but not for submission. They want AI efficiency + human accountability.

So that's what we built: GrantPilot pairs AI automation with human specialist QA before every submission.

The platform is live at https://app-limalabs.vercel.app and free to try.

What are you finding in your grant writing process? Would love to hear from nonprofit and municipal teams.

#customerresearch #nonprofit #productdevelopment #grantwriting

We just published 58 free grant narrative templates. No login required.

Covers the 14 most common federal and foundation funders:
• HUD CDBG (housing, public services, economic dev)
• SAMHSA (behavioral health, prevention)
• USDA Rural Development
• DOJ (VOCA victims services, Byrne JAG)
• RWJF, Annie E. Casey, MacArthur, Kresge foundations
• NEH preservation and humanities
• EPA Environmental Justice
• NSF broader impacts
• AmeriCorps VISTA
• HHS Head Start
• Title I education
• HRSA FQHCs
• State CSBG, Housing Trust Fund

Each template includes word-limit guidance and metadata from thousands of funded applications.

Free at: https://app-limalabs.vercel.app/resources

If you're a grant writer or nonprofit ED — what templates would you add? Drop them in the comments.

#grantwriting #nonprofit #federalgrants #templates #philanthropy

youtube.title: What is a Grant Win Score? How GrantPilot Predicts Grant Success
youtube.description:
Before you spend 40 hours on a grant application, know if you're competitive.

GrantPilot's Win Score analyzes 6 factors:
1. Eligibility Match (25% weight) - do you actually qualify?
2. Narrative Completeness (20%) - how much have you drafted?
3. Budget Quality (15%) - is your budget OMB-compliant?
4. Compliance & Forms (15%) - are required forms addressed?
5. Organization Profile (10%) - is your profile complete?
6. Human QA Review (15%) - has a specialist reviewed it?

Each factor gets a 0-100 score. The weighted composite tells you: submit, improve first, or skip.

Score 80+: Submit with confidence
Score 60-79: Good — a few improvements needed
Score 40-59: Developing — core sections needed
Score <40: Early stage — get the basics done first

Try GrantPilot free: https://app-limalabs.vercel.app

#GrantWriting #Nonprofit #AI #Grants #WinScore
